STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[9]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_9.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 9: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".

- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-arch-011 (#1268) — BEHAVIORAL breadth preflight (the §-1.4 replay gate for F2a).
3	
4	Proves, on the BANKED drb_78 corpus_snapshot (794 generator-visible rows) and WITHOUT
5	any LLM / VM spend, that the F2a guard fix actually restores the 794->9 breadth collapse.
6	FAILS LOUD (non-zero exit) if any tier does not fire — "committed + green != wired"
7	(CLAUDE.md §-1.4); the acceptance is the EFFECT APPEARING in the real selection, not a
8	diff approval.
9	
10	Why this is LLM-free and faithful (the exact run condition):
11	  * The Gate-B run sets PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_JUDGE=off -> the credibility judge arrives None,
12	    so ``run_credibility_analysis`` runs PRIORS-ONLY (ZERO scoring LLM calls).
13	  * The Gate-B slate does NOT set PG_VERIFICATION_MODE (it stays "off"), so the per-member
14	    basket verify (``verify_sentence_provenance``) makes ZERO entailment LLM calls.
15	  Both judges therefore dormant -> the whole basket->enrichment back-half is DETERMINISTIC.
16	
17	The tiers:
18	  T0 GUARD   — ``_credibility_guard_decision(judge=None, gov_suffixes=<real>, always_release=True)``
19	               returns "run" (the F2a fix). Pre-fix it returned "degrade" -> basket never built
20	               -> the 794->9 collapse. This is the one-line behavioral assertion of the fix.
21	  T1 BASKET  — the REAL ``run_credibility_analysis(judge=None)`` on the 794 banked rows COMPLETES
22	               (settles B12-COMPLETION's "hundreds of calls then fail" fear — false on the
23	               priors-only path) and CONSOLIDATES (>= 1 basket carries > 1 supporting member).
24	  T2 SURFACE — ``diagnose_unbound_supports_selection`` returns reason=="ok" and an unbound-SUPPORTS
25	               candidate list FAR larger than 9 (pre-F2a this returned reason="credibility_analysis_none",
26	               len 0). This is the breadth the enrichment section OFFERS.
27	  T3 RENDER  — ``build_verified_span_draft`` emits a non-empty deterministic verbatim-span draft and the
28	               number of DISTINCT sources that contribute a citable unit (the rendered-count upper bound
29	               before the unchanged strict_verify gate at section render).
30	
31	Faithfulness is NEVER relaxed: every surfaced source is re-verified against its own span by the
32	UNCHANGED strict_verify at render; this harness only proves the candidates are OFFERED (pre-fix
33	they were structurally withheld). It does not move any verify gate.
34	
35	Usage:
36	    python scripts/iarch011_breadth_preflight.py \
37	        [--corpus outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/drb_78_parkinsons_dbs/corpus_snapshot.json] \
38	        [--min-ev-ids 50]
39	
40	Exit 0 == GO (breadth restored). Non-zero == NO-GO (do NOT spend the VM run).
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import argparse
46	import json
47	import os
48	import re
49	import sys
50	import time
51	
52	# Repo root on sys.path so ``src.*`` imports resolve when run as ``python scripts/...``.
53	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))
54	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
55	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
56	
57	# MEMBERSHIP-CEILING measurement (entailment OFF in THIS harness only — NOT in production).
58	# F2a-alone ships the basket verify under entailment-ENFORCE (the PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT
59	# default; F2b was reverted). Running 767 real entailment LLM calls locally would be slow + costly,
60	# so this harness sets entailment OFF to measure the MECHANICAL membership CEILING fast and free:
61	# it proves the guard runs, the basket BUILDS, and the breadth enrichment SURFACES candidates.
62	# The entailment-enforced run will CULL this ceiling down (a member whose span does not entail the
63	# claim is dropped) — so T2/T3 here are an UPPER BOUND; the real entailment-enforced cited count is
64	# proven on the VM (16-way bounded-parallel under the 3000s wall). This is HONEST, not a relaxation:
65	# production keeps enforce; only this offline ceiling probe disables it.
66	os.environ["PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT"] = "off"
67	
68	_DEFAULT_CORPUS = (
69	    "outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/drb_78_parkinsons_dbs/corpus_snapshot.json"
70	)
71	_EV_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[([^\[\]]+)\]")
72	
73	
74	def _fail(msg: str) -> None:
75	    print(f"\n[PREFLIGHT][NO-GO] {msg}", flush=True)
76	    sys.exit(1)
77	
78	
79	def main() -> None:
80	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="I-arch-011 breadth preflight (F2a)")
81	    ap.add_argument("--corpus", default=_DEFAULT_CORPUS)
82	    ap.add_argument(
83	        "--min-ev-ids", type=int, default=50,
84	        help="minimum unbound-SUPPORTS candidates required to declare GO (>9 is the literal "
85	             "collapse threshold; 50 is a conservative breadth bar)",
86	    )
87	    args = ap.parse_args()
88	
89	    if not os.path.exists(args.corpus):
90	        _fail(f"corpus_snapshot not found: {args.corpus}")
91	
92	    print(f"[PREFLIGHT] loading {args.corpus}", flush=True)
93	    snap = json.load(open(args.corpus, encoding="utf-8"))
94	    rows = snap.get("evidence_for_gen") or []
95	    question = snap.get("question") or ""
96	    domain = snap.get("domain") or None
97	    if not rows:
98	        _fail("corpus_snapshot has no evidence_for_gen rows")
99	    # Mirror production: evidence_pool = {evidence_id: row}; pass its values to the pass.
100	    evidence_pool = {}
101	    for r in rows:
102	        eid = str((r or {}).get("evidence_id") or "").strip()
103	        if eid:
104	            evidence_pool.setdefault(eid, r)
105	    pool_rows = list(evidence_pool.values())
106	    print(
107	        f"[PREFLIGHT] rows={len(rows)} distinct_ev_ids={len(evidence_pool)} "
108	        f"domain={domain!r} q='{question[:70]}...'",
109	        flush=True,
110	    )
111	
112	    from src.polaris_graph.authority.data_loader import load_authority_data
113	    gov = tuple(load_authority_data().get("psl_gov_suffixes") or ())
114	    if not gov:
115	        _fail("psl_gov_suffixes empty (authority data did not load)")
116	    print(f"[PREFLIGHT] psl_gov_suffixes={len(gov)}", flush=True)
117	
118	    # ── T0 GUARD ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
119	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import (
120	        _credibility_guard_decision,
121	    )
122	    decision = _credibility_guard_decision(
123	        judge=None, gov_suffixes=gov, always_release=True,
124	    )
125	    print(f"\n[T0 GUARD] judge=None, gov_suffixes present, always_release=True -> {decision!r}")
126	    if decision != "run":
127	        _fail(
128	            f"F2a guard regression: expected 'run' (priors-only basket build), got {decision!r}. "
129	            f"Pre-F2a this was 'degrade' -> credibility_analysis=None -> 794->9 collapse."
130	        )
131	    # And the legacy fail-closed posture is preserved when always_release is OFF.
132	    legacy = _credibility_guard_decision(judge=None, gov_suffixes=gov, always_release=False)
133	    if legacy != "raise":
134	        _fail(f"legacy always-release-OFF posture broken: expected 'raise', got {legacy!r}")
135	    # gov_suffixes-missing still degrades/raises (intact, NOT collapsed into the judge case).
136	    govmiss = _credibility_guard_decision(judge=None, gov_suffixes=(), always_release=True)
137	    if govmiss != "degrade":
138	        _fail(f"gov_suffixes-missing branch broken: expected 'degrade', got {govmiss!r}")
139	    print("[T0 GUARD] PASS — judge=None now RUNS priors-only; legacy + gov-missing branches intact")
140	
141	    # ── T1 BASKET ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
142	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.credibility_pass import run_credibility_analysis
143	    print("\n[T1 BASKET] running REAL run_credibility_analysis(judge=None) on the banked rows "
144	          "(entailment-OFF in this harness = MEMBERSHIP CEILING; prod ships enforce) ...",
145	          flush=True)
146	    # Watchdog: the advisory basket verify previously hung on a SERIAL per-member entailment
147	    # network call (767 members). If F2b did not neutralize it, this never returns — so run it
148	    # in a worker thread and FAIL LOUD on a 120s join timeout instead of hanging the harness.
149	    import threading
150	    _result: dict = {}
151	    def _run():
152	        try:
153	            _result["analysis"] = run_credibility_analysis(
154	                question, pool_rows, gov_suffixes=gov, domain=domain, judge=None,
155	            )
156	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — B12's "then fail" condition; capture + surface
157	            import traceback
158	            _result["error"] = exc
159	            _result["tb"] = traceback.format_exc()
160	    t0 = time.time()
161	    th = threading.Thread(target=_run, daemon=True)
162	    th.start()
163	    th.join(timeout=120)
164	    elapsed = time.time() - t0
165	    if th.is_alive():
166	        _fail(
167	            f"run_credibility_analysis(judge=None) DID NOT RETURN in {elapsed:.0f}s — the advisory "
168	            f"per-member entailment HANG is NOT neutralized. F2b ineffective; do NOT spend the VM run."
169	        )
170	    if "error" in _result:
171	        print(_result.get("tb", ""))
172	        _fail(f"run_credibility_analysis(judge=None) RAISED "
173	              f"({type(_result['error']).__name__}: {_result['error']}) — B12's 'then fail' materialized")
174	    analysis = _result["analysis"]
175	    baskets = list(getattr(analysis, "baskets", None) or [])
176	    member_counts = [
177	        len(getattr(b, "supporting_members", None) or ()) for b in baskets
178	    ]
179	    multi = sum(1 for c in member_counts if c > 1)
180	    max_members = max(member_counts) if member_counts else 0
181	    total_support = 0
182	    for b in baskets:
183	        for m in (getattr(b, "supporting_members", None) or ()):
184	            if str(getattr(m, "span_verdict", "")).strip().upper() == "SUPPORTS":
185	                total_support += 1
186	    print(
187	        f"[T1 BASKET] completed in {elapsed:.1f}s | baskets={len(baskets)} "
188	        f"multi_member_baskets={multi} max_members={max_members} total_SUPPORTS_members={total_support}"
189	    )
190	    if not baskets:
191	        _fail("run_credibility_analysis(judge=None) produced ZERO baskets — basket never built")
192	    if multi < 1:
193	        _fail(
194	            "consolidation did NOT fire: no basket carries >1 supporting member "
195	            f"(max_members={max_members}). The breadth lever needs multi-source baskets."
196	        )
197	    print("[T1 BASKET] PASS — priors-only pass COMPLETES fast and CONSOLIDATES (multi-source baskets)")
198	
199	    # ── T2 SURFACE ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
200	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (
201	        diagnose_unbound_supports_selection,
202	    )
203	    # contract_plans=[] -> excluded_bound=0 -> UPPER bound on surfaced breadth (the real run
204	    # excludes only the ~5 contract-bound entities, a negligible reduction). Honest approximation.
205	    wfe = diagnose_unbound_supports_selection(
206	        evidence_pool=evidence_pool, credibility_analysis=analysis, contract_plans=[],
207	    )
208	    print(
209	        f"\n[T2 SURFACE] reason={wfe.reason!r} ev_ids={len(wfe.ev_ids)} "
210	        f"baskets_seen={wfe.baskets_seen} supports_members_seen={wfe.supports_members_seen} "
211	        f"excluded_bound={wfe.excluded_bound} pool_absent={wfe.excluded_pool_absent} "
212	        f"below_floor_kept(telemetry)={wfe.excluded_below_floor}"
213	    )
214	    if wfe.reason != "ok":
215	        _fail(
216	            f"breadth enrichment would be EMPTY (reason={wfe.reason!r}). Pre-F2a this was "
217	            f"'credibility_analysis_none' — if still empty, F2a did not restore the basket."
218	        )
219	    if len(wfe.ev_ids) <= 9:
220	        _fail(
221	            f"unbound-SUPPORTS candidates ({len(wfe.ev_ids)}) did not climb past the collapse "
222	            f"threshold (9). F2a did not widen breadth."
223	        )
224	    if len(wfe.ev_ids) < args.min_ev_ids:
225	        _fail(
226	            f"unbound-SUPPORTS candidates ({len(wfe.ev_ids)}) below the breadth bar "
227	            f"(--min-ev-ids={args.min_ev_ids}). Investigate finding_dedup consolidation (F1) or "
228	            f"PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR ordering — surgically, NEVER a cap/floor/target (§-1.3)."
229	        )
230	    print(f"[T2 SURFACE] PASS — {len(wfe.ev_ids)} unbound-SUPPORTS candidates surfaced (was 0 pre-F2a)")
231	
232	    # ── T3 RENDER (upper bound, deterministic) ───────────────────────────────────
233	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import build_verified_span_draft
234	    # FIX-K is the actual render mode (PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_RENDER_VERIFIED_SPANS=1 on the slate).
235	    draft = build_verified_span_draft(wfe.ev_ids, evidence_pool)
236	    marker_ids = set(_EV_MARKER_RE.findall(draft or ""))
237	    contributing = {mid for mid in marker_ids if mid in evidence_pool}
238	    print(
239	        f"\n[T3 RENDER] verified-span draft len={len(draft)} chars | "
240	        f"distinct contributing sources (pre-strict_verify)={len(contributing)}"
241	    )
242	    if not draft.strip():
243	        _fail("build_verified_span_draft produced an EMPTY draft — nothing would render")
244	    if len(contributing) <= 9:
245	        _fail(
246	            f"only {len(contributing)} sources contribute a citable verbatim unit (<=9). The "
247	            f"rendered cited-source count would not climb. Investigate the junk-screen / unit split."
248	        )
249	    print(f"[T3 RENDER] PASS — {len(contributing)} sources contribute a deterministic citable unit")
250	
251	    # ── VERDICT ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
252	    print("\n" + "=" * 72)
253	    print("[PREFLIGHT][GO] F2a (entailment-ENFORCE in prod) RESTORES the breadth CEILING on drb_78:")
254	    print(f"   T0 guard judge=None -> 'run' (priors-only basket build)")
255	    print(f"   T1 priors-only pass: {elapsed:.1f}s, {multi} multi-source baskets (max {max_members})")
256	    print(f"   T2 unbound-SUPPORTS CEILING: {len(wfe.ev_ids)}  (collapse was 9)")
257	    print(f"   T3 deterministic render CEILING: {len(contributing)} distinct sources")
258	    print("   NOTE: T2/T3 are the entailment-OFF MEMBERSHIP CEILING (this harness only). Production")
259	    print("   ships entailment-ENFORCE (16-way bounded, 3000s wall); the enforced run CULLS this")
260	    print("   ceiling to the real cited count — proven on the VM, not here. Faithfulness NEVER relaxed:")
261	    print("   every cited corroborator is entailment-verified by the basket verify under enforce.")
262	    print("=" * 72)
263	    sys.exit(0)
264	
265	
266	if __name__ == "__main__":
267	    main()

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-arch-011 PR-e — CONSOLIDATED composition behavioral replay gate (§-1.4, FAIL LOUD).
3	
4	This is the single pre-spend gate for the composition rebuild: it runs EVERY locally-replayable
5	component behavioral harness on a REAL banked ``corpus_snapshot.json`` (with ``PG_PRD_REQUIRE_REAL=1``
6	so a missing corpus FAILS rather than silently falling back to a synthetic fixture), and exits non-zero
7	naming the first failure. It does NOT relax faithfulness or add production code — it orchestrates the
8	already-Codex-approved per-component harnesses so the whole composition back-half can be gated with one
9	command before any VM spend.
10	
11	Components run here (each internally exercises enforce + hermetic ``PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT``):
12	  - I-arch-010 TAIL          -> scripts/iarch010_replay_breadth_faithfulness_harness.py
13	  - I-arch-011 PR-b BASKETS  -> scripts/iarch011_prb_corroboration_replay_harness.py
14	  - I-arch-011 PR-c COMPOSE  -> scripts/iarch011_prc_verified_compose_replay_harness.py
15	  - I-arch-011 PR-d ABS/CONC -> scripts/iarch011_prd_abstract_conclusion_replay_harness.py
16	
17	NOT locally replayable (explicitly disclosed — never silently omitted; covered by the FINAL fresh VM
18	run + its §-1.1 line-by-line audit, per state/iarch_wiring_acceptance_checklist.md):
19	  - I-arch-009 QUALITY GATE (Qwen3-Embedding-8B relevance embedder) — needs the 8B model on a GPU; its
20	    behavioral proof is the GPU bake-off (scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py) + the VM run's
21	    fail-closed loaded-identity == Qwen3-Embedding-8B check (no silent MiniLM).
22	  - I-arch-011 PR-a STORM-OUTLINE adapter — structure-only (asserts no facts); its section-scaffold
23	    wiring fires end-to-end only in a full run, verified on the VM (sections == STORM outline).
24	
25	Run: ``python scripts/iarch011_composition_replay_harness.py`` -> exit 0 iff EVERY component harness
26	passes on real corpus; non-zero + the failing component otherwise.
27	"""
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import json
31	import os
32	import subprocess
33	import sys
34	from pathlib import Path
35	
36	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
37	
38	# Load C:/POLARIS/.env so the live entailment judge is reachable for the enforce legs.
39	try:
40	    from dotenv import load_dotenv  # type: ignore
41	
42	    load_dotenv(_REPO_ROOT / ".env")
43	except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — dotenv optional; env may already be exported
44	    pass
45	
46	# Per-harness wall-clock cap (s). Each child has its OWN internal bounded deadline + fail-loud, so this
47	# is a backstop; a child that exceeds it is a HANG and FAILS the gate (never silently passes).
48	_PER_HARNESS_TIMEOUT_S = 420
49	
50	_COMPONENTS = [
51	    ("I-arch-010 TAIL (breadth + faithfulness)", "scripts/iarch010_replay_breadth_faithfulness_harness.py"),
52	    ("I-arch-011 PR-b BASKETS (keep-all corroboration)", "scripts/iarch011_prb_corroboration_replay_harness.py"),
53	    ("I-arch-011 PR-c COMPOSE (per-basket verified-compose)", "scripts/iarch011_prc_verified_compose_replay_harness.py"),
54	    ("I-arch-011 PR-d ABSTRACT/CONCLUSION (verbatim synthesis)", "scripts/iarch011_prd_abstract_conclusion_replay_harness.py"),
55	]
56	
57	# Components that CANNOT be replayed on this box — disclosed in the output, NEVER silently dropped.
58	_NOT_LOCALLY_REPLAYABLE = [
59	    "I-arch-009 QUALITY GATE (Qwen3-Embedding-8B) — GPU bake-off + VM loaded-identity fail-closed check",
60	    "I-arch-011 PR-a STORM-OUTLINE adapter — structure-only; section-scaffold verified on the VM run",
61	]
62	
63	
64	def _run_component(label: str, rel_path: str, env: dict) -> dict:
65	    script = _REPO_ROOT / rel_path
66	    if not script.is_file():
67	        return {"label": label, "script": rel_path, "ok": False,
68	                "reason": "harness script MISSING from the stack"}
69	    try:
70	        proc = subprocess.run(
71	            [sys.executable, str(script)],
72	            cwd=str(_REPO_ROOT),
73	            env=env,
74	            capture_output=True,
75	            text=True,
76	            timeout=_PER_HARNESS_TIMEOUT_S,
77	        )
78	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
79	        return {"label": label, "script": rel_path, "ok": False,
80	                "reason": f"HANG: exceeded {_PER_HARNESS_TIMEOUT_S}s (a child harness must fail loud, never hang)"}
81	    ok = proc.returncode == 0
82	    tail = (proc.stderr or proc.stdout or "").strip().splitlines()
83	    last = tail[-1] if tail else ""
84	    return {"label": label, "script": rel_path, "ok": ok, "returncode": proc.returncode,
85	            "last_line": last[:300]}
86	
87	
88	def main() -> int:
89	    # Enforce REAL corpus across the children (a missing banked snapshot FAILS, never silently synthetic).
90	    env = dict(os.environ)
91	    env["PG_PRD_REQUIRE_REAL"] = "1"
92	
93	    results = [_run_component(label, path, env) for label, path in _COMPONENTS]
94	    failed = [r for r in results if not r["ok"]]
95	
96	    print(json.dumps({
97	        "harness": "iarch011_composition_replay_harness (PR-e consolidated gate)",
98	        "status": "PASS" if not failed else "FAIL",
99	        "components_run": results,
100	        "not_locally_replayable_disclosed": _NOT_LOCALLY_REPLAYABLE,
101	        "require_real_corpus": True,
102	        "faithfulness": "untouched — orchestrates the per-component harnesses; no production code, no relaxation",
103	    }, indent=2))
104	
105	    if failed:
106	        for r in failed:
107	            print(f"FAIL [{r['label']}]: {r.get('reason') or r.get('last_line')!r} "
108	                  f"(rc={r.get('returncode')})", file=sys.stderr)
109	        return 1
110	    return 0
111	
112	
113	if __name__ == "__main__":
114	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python
2	"""I-arch-011 entailment-deadline DEADLOCK discriminator + §-1.4 regression harness.
3	
4	Advisor-mandated DIAGNOSTIC-FIRST (run #6 froze 13 min at the 737-source enrichment-section
5	verify, stack parked in entailment_judge._post_with_total_deadline:127 fut.result()). A bounded
6	single judge() call should cap at ~2*PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S then emit the fail-closed sentinel, so a
7	13-min freeze is anomalous. This harness points the REAL _EntailmentJudge at a LOCAL server that
8	accepts the connection then TRICKLES body bytes forever (never completing) — the exact
9	"keep-alive resets the per-read gap timer" wedge HANG-J3 describes — and discriminates:
10	
11	  (a) total_s not honored / wrong value -> _post_with_total_deadline never bounds (P1 hangs)
12	  (b) deadline fires but client.close()/retry/rebuild loops forever  (P1 or P2 hangs)
13	  (c) cross-call client poisoning / leaked wedged worker            (P3 hangs)
14	
15	FAIL-LOUD: exits non-zero if ANY probe exceeds WALL (= MULT * total_s). After the fix EVERY probe
16	must bound. This file is BOTH the diagnostic and the banked regression test (re-run post-fix; it
17	must turn all-GREEN). Real code path, zero mocks of the judge transport.
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import os
22	import socket
23	import sys
24	import threading
25	import time
26	
27	# ---- knobs (small so the repro is fast; LAW VI env-overridable) -------------------------------
28	TOTAL_S = float(os.environ.get("REPRO_TOTAL_S", "5"))          # the per-call deadline under test
29	WALL_MULT = float(os.environ.get("REPRO_WALL_MULT", "6"))      # a probe exceeding MULT*deadline = UNBOUNDED
30	WALL_S = TOTAL_S * WALL_MULT
31	MODE = os.environ.get("REPRO_MODE", "trickle")                 # trickle | silent
32	
33	
34	def _log(msg: str) -> None:
35	    print(f"[repro {time.strftime('%H:%M:%S')}] {msg}", flush=True)
36	
37	
38	class WedgeServer:
39	    """A local TCP server that accepts, reads the request, then NEVER completes the response.
40	
41	    trickle: send 200 + a huge Content-Length, then drip 1 body byte every 2 s forever — httpx's
42	             per-read gap timer resets on each byte, so a non-streaming .post() read blocks
43	             UNBOUNDED (the production OpenRouter/Cloudflare keep-alive trickle).
44	    silent:  send NOTHING after accept (a truly dead rx=tx=0 socket).
45	    """
46	
47	    def __init__(self, mode: str) -> None:
48	        self.mode = mode
49	        self._srv = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
50	        self._srv.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
51	        self._srv.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
52	        self._srv.listen(64)
53	        self.port = self._srv.getsockname()[1]
54	        self._stop = False
55	        self._conns: list[socket.socket] = []
56	        self._t = threading.Thread(target=self._serve, daemon=True)
57	
58	    def start(self) -> None:
59	        self._t.start()
60	
61	    def _serve(self) -> None:
62	        while not self._stop:
63	            try:
64	                self._srv.settimeout(1.0)
65	                conn, _ = self._srv.accept()
66	            except socket.timeout:
67	                continue
68	            except OSError:
69	                break
70	            self._conns.append(conn)
71	            threading.Thread(target=self._handle, args=(conn,), daemon=True).start()
72	
73	    def _handle(self, conn: socket.socket) -> None:
74	        try:
75	            conn.settimeout(2.0)
76	            try:
77	                conn.recv(65536)  # drain request headers (best-effort)
78	            except OSError:
79	                pass
80	            if self.mode == "silent":
81	                while not self._stop:
82	                    time.sleep(0.5)  # hold open, send nothing
83	                return
84	            # trickle: valid status + a Content-Length we will NEVER satisfy, then drip forever
85	            head = (
86	                "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n"
87	                "Content-Type: application/json\r\n"
88	                "Content-Length: 1000000000\r\n"
89	                "Connection: keep-alive\r\n"
90	                "\r\n"
91	            )
92	            conn.sendall(head.encode())
93	            while not self._stop:
94	                try:
95	                    conn.sendall(b" ")  # one keep-alive byte: resets httpx's read-gap timer
96	                except OSError:
97	                    return
98	                time.sleep(2.0)
99	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
100	            _log(f"server handler exit: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
101	
102	    def close(self) -> None:
103	        self._stop = True
104	        for c in self._conns:
105	            try:
106	                c.close()
107	            except OSError:
108	                pass
109	        try:
110	            self._srv.close()
111	        except OSError:
112	            pass
113	
114	
115	def _run_probe(name: str, fn) -> dict:
116	    """Run fn() in a daemon thread, join up to WALL_S. Returns timing + bounded/unbounded verdict."""
117	    result: dict = {"name": name, "outcome": None, "elapsed": None, "bounded": None}
118	    start = time.monotonic()
119	
120	    def _target() -> None:
121	        try:
122	            r = fn()
123	            result["outcome"] = f"returned: {r!r}"
124	        except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — we WANT to see TimeoutError etc.
125	            result["outcome"] = f"raised: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
126	        finally:
127	            result["elapsed"] = time.monotonic() - start
128	
129	    t = threading.Thread(target=_target, daemon=True)
130	    t.start()
131	    t.join(WALL_S)
132	    if t.is_alive():
133	        result["elapsed"] = time.monotonic() - start
134	        result["outcome"] = f"STILL RUNNING after {WALL_S:.0f}s (leaked)"
135	        result["bounded"] = False
136	        _log(f"  {name}: UNBOUNDED — {result['outcome']}")
137	    else:
138	        result["bounded"] = result["elapsed"] <= WALL_S
139	        _log(f"  {name}: {result['outcome']}  (elapsed {result['elapsed']:.1f}s, "
140	             f"{'BOUNDED' if result['bounded'] else 'UNBOUNDED'})")
141	    return result
142	
143	
144	def main() -> int:
145	    # env so the REAL _EntailmentJudge constructs + points at the local wedge server
146	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL", "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro")
147	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.1")  # diff family from generator
148	    os.environ["OPENROUTER_API_KEY"] = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "sk-repro-dummy")
149	    os.environ["PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S"] = str(TOTAL_S)
150	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES", "1")  # the run-slate value
151	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_ENTAILMENT_RETRIES", "2")
152	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_ENTAILMENT_RETRY_BACKOFF_S", "0.2")
153	
154	    srv = WedgeServer(MODE)
155	    srv.start()
156	    os.environ["OPENROUTER_BASE_URL"] = f"http://127.0.0.1:{srv.port}/api/v1"
157	    _log(f"wedge server up on 127.0.0.1:{srv.port} mode={MODE}; TOTAL_S={TOTAL_S} WALL_S={WALL_S}")
158	
159	    # import AFTER env is set (module reads PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S at import)
160	    import importlib
161	    ej = importlib.import_module("src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge")
162	    importlib.reload(ej)  # ensure _ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S picks up our TOTAL_S
163	    _log(f"module _ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S = {ej._ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_S} (must equal {TOTAL_S})")

3	
4	Acceptance is BEHAVIORAL: the per-claim basket-corroboration block (PR-b) must ACTUALLY
5	APPEAR in the REAL rendered ``report.md`` bibliography output, driven by the PRODUCTION
6	``PG_BASKET_CORROBORATION_RENDER`` env flag — not "Codex approved the diff" and not "tests
7	are green" (CLAUDE.md §-1.4). This closes the Codex P1 on the prior PR-b commit (2849a29f),
8	which had the render work correct but lacked the §-1.4 behavioral replay over a real-shaped
9	ClaimBasket through the REAL render path.
10	
11	Why no real on-disk corpus_snapshot.json: the I-arch-007/010 corpus backups
12	(``outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/<slug>/corpus_snapshot.json``) are gitignored and NOT
13	checked out into a fresh worktree (verified absent at build time). The I-arch-010
14	replay-harness handles the identical situation by SYNTHESIZING the real-shaped objects and
15	driving the REAL production functions through them; this harness follows that sanctioned
16	fallback. The objects are real ``ClaimBasket`` / ``BasketMember`` instances (NOT hand-baked
17	dicts) flowing through the REAL ``resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count`` ->
18	``_basket_for_biblio`` projection -> ``_render_bibliography_lines`` chain — the same call
19	chain ``multi_section_generator`` uses for ``report.md`` — so the EFFECT is exercised in the
20	production render path, not mocked.
21	
22	The wiring proof (the heart of the Codex P1): the harness does NOT pass
23	``corroboration_render=True`` literally. It sets ``PG_BASKET_CORROBORATION_RENDER=1`` and
24	reads the flag through ``sweep._env_flag(sweep._BASKET_CORROBORATION_RENDER_ENV, ...)`` — the
25	EXACT helper + identifier the LIVE call site (run_one_query) uses — then passes that resolved
26	bool into ``_render_bibliography_lines``. So the assertions prove env-flag -> real output, the
27	§-1.4 "fired in the output, not config" property, not merely that the function accepts a kwarg.
28	
29	FAITHFULNESS: this harness adds NO production code. It reads-only. ``strict_verify`` /
30	``_classify_member_tier`` / the verified-support invariant (only ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED counts)
31	are untouched. The basket is constructed with a DETERMINISTIC_ONLY weak member, an UNVERIFIED
32	garbage member, and a contradict/refuter reference so the harness PROVES, in the rendered
33	output, that a weak member is surfaced LABELED-weak but NEVER counted as verified support and
34	NEVER rendered as a verified citation (no inflation).

36	Run: ``python scripts/iarch011_prb_corroboration_replay_harness.py`` -> exit 0 if every
37	assertion fires, non-zero + the failing assertion on any miss.

78	def _build_real_basket_inputs():

111	        BasketMember("ev_c", _WEAK_URL, "T4", "o3", 0.40, 0.3, (0, 8),

113	        BasketMember("ev_d", _GARBAGE_URL, "T7", "o4", 0.05, 0.05, (0, 4),
114	                     "news", "UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_UNVERIFIED),
115	    ]

118	        "reduced HbA1c", members, ("c9",), 2.7, 4, 2, "contested",
119	    )

121	    return kept, evidence_pool, [basket], cluster_id_by_evidence

125	    kept, evidence_pool, baskets, cluster_id_by_evidence = _build_real_basket_inputs()
126	
127	    # ── Drive the REAL resolver: it attaches row["baskets"] via _basket_for_biblio exactly as
128	    # the live report.md path (multi_section_generator) does. NOT a hand-baked row dict. ──
129	    _text, biblio, _emitted = resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count(
130	        kept, evidence_pool, baskets=baskets,

132	    )

139	    # the member_tier seam (I-arch-010) must survive the projection — it is what the render reads

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-arch-011 #1268 PR-c — behavioral replay harness for per-basket VERIFIED-COMPOSE.
3	
4	Fail-loud (non-zero exit) acceptance for the CONTRACT (composition_design_plan.md PR-c):
5	per basket the writer drafts prose -> deterministic strict_verify each sentence against the
6	BASKET-SCOPED pool -> a passing sentence is kept; a FAILING sentence (incl. one citing ANOTHER
7	basket's evidence_id, the P1-2 anti-cross-claim case) FALLS BACK to THAT BASKET'S OWN verified
8	K-span; never empty.
9	
10	THREE DISCRIMINATING FIXTURES (verbatim from the design):
11	  c1  the writer returns marker-less garbage (FORCED compose FAIL)  -> basket-1's OWN verbatim K-span.
12	  c2  the writer returns a faithful marker-bearing sentence (= c2's own span + ``[#ev:c2]``) -> PASSES,
13	      composed prose renders.
14	  c3  the writer returns c2's faithful sentence — content-faithful to the FOREIGN basket, citing
15	      ``[#ev:c2]`` (a FOREIGN id for basket-3). Under basket-3's BASKET-SCOPED pool (only c3's id)
16	      ``[#ev:c2]`` is ABSENT -> strict_verify REJECTS it -> basket-3 falls back to its OWN K-span.
17	      (If the compose pool were the FULL pool, the foreign sentence would PASS and basket-3 would
18	      render c2's foreign content — the exact P1-2 regression this fixture discriminates.)
19	
20	The LLM writer is FAKE (injected); strict_verify is the REAL production single-sentence verifier
21	(``verify_sentence_provenance``). No network, no model spend, no relaxation.
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations

29	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]

79	# scoped pool + acceptance regions MUST be built from SUPPORTS-only members; if they admit an UNSUPPORTED
80	# member's evidence_id+span, a writer that cites that member renders unverified text AS verified. The
81	# fixture: a basket with one SUPPORTS member and one UNSUPPORTED member; the writer cites the UNSUPPORTED
82	# member's own (real-row-resolvable) span. EXPECTED (post-fix): the sentence is REJECTED (the unsupported
83	# member defines NO scoped-pool entry / acceptance region) and the basket falls back to its SUPPORTS
84	# K-span. RED (current code): the unsupported member is in the pool+region, so the sentence PASSES.
85	_P1_SUPPORTS_QUOTE = "Coastal wetland restoration increased measured carbon sequestration rates."

99	    falls back to its SUPPORTS K-span. RED on the holed code (the unsupported member is pooled+regioned)."""
100	    eid_ok = "ev_p1_supports"

103	        claim_cluster_id="p1c", claim_text=_P1_SUPPORTS_QUOTE, subject="wetland carbon", predicate="finding",

266	    k3 = build_verified_span_draft(c3, evidence_pool)
267	    if k1 is None or "ev_c1" not in k1 or k3 is None or "ev_c3" not in k3:
268	        _fail("kspan", f"verbatim K-span draft did not bind to the basket's own id: k1={k1!r} k3={k3!r}")
269	
270	    # ── c1: FORCED compose-fail -> basket-1's OWN verbatim K-span (its quote + its own id). ──
271	    if "[#ev:ev_c1:" not in r1 or _C1_QUOTE.rstrip(".") not in r1:
272	        _fail("c1_own_kspan", f"c1 must fall back to its OWN verbatim K-span; got {r1!r}")
273	    if "ev_c2" in r1 or "ev_c3" in r1:
274	        _fail("c1_no_foreign", f"c1 fallback leaked a foreign basket id; got {r1!r}")
275	
276	    # ── c2: faithful compose PASSES -> the composed sentence renders (c2's span + c2's id). ──
277	    if "[#ev:ev_c2:" not in r2 or _C2_QUOTE.rstrip(".") not in r2:
278	        _fail("c2_pass", f"c2's faithful composed sentence must render; got {r2!r}")
279	
280	    # ── c3: P1-2 — foreign-cited compose REJECTED under the basket-scoped pool -> c3's OWN K-span. ──
281	    if "[#ev:ev_c2:" in r3:
282	        _fail("c3_p1_2", f"P1-2 VIOLATION: c3 rendered the FOREIGN [#ev:ev_c2] compose; got {r3!r}")
283	    if "[#ev:ev_c3:" not in r3 or _C3_QUOTE.rstrip(".") not in r3:
284	        _fail("c3_own_kspan", f"c3 must fall back to its OWN verbatim K-span after rejecting the foreign cite; got {r3!r}")
285	
286	    # ── P1-1 HARDENING (Codex core-gate): the HARDER cross-claim case — a SHARED source backs two
287	    # baskets with DIFFERENT claim-specific spans. cluster_id_by_evidence is 1-to-many, so a naive
288	    # evidence_id-only scope would let basket C render basket B's claim citing the shared id (the id
289	    # IS in C's pool). The fix pins each scoped row to the MEMBER'S OWN span, so the cross-claim
290	    # sentence fails to ground -> C falls back to its OWN claim-specific span.
291	    _shared = "ev_shared"
292	    _b_span = "Tariff reductions lowered consumer electronics prices."
293	    _c_span = "Vaccine coverage rose among rural adolescent populations."
294	    _full = f"{_b_span} {_c_span}"                          # the GLOBAL source text (both claims)
295	    _b_off = (0, len(_b_span))
296	    _c_off = (len(_b_span) + 1, len(_full))
297	
298	    def _shared_member(span_off, quote) -> BasketMember:
299	        return BasketMember(
300	            evidence_id=_shared, source_url="https://example.org/shared", source_tier="T1",
301	            origin_cluster_id="o::shared", credibility_weight=0.9, authority_score=0.9,
302	            span=span_off, direct_quote=quote, span_verdict="SUPPORTS",
303	            member_tier=MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED,
304	        )
305	
306	    c_shared = ClaimBasket(
307	        claim_cluster_id="c_shared", claim_text=_c_span, subject="vaccines", predicate="finding",
308	        supporting_members=[_shared_member(_c_off, _c_span)], refuter_cluster_ids=(), weight_mass=1.0,
309	        total_clustered_origin_count=1, verified_support_origin_count=1, basket_verdict="full",
310	    )
311	    # The GLOBAL pool row is the FULL source text — its [_b_off] is B's claim, [_c_off] is C's.
312	    pool_shared = {_shared: {"evidence_id": _shared, "direct_quote": _full}}
313	
314	    def _writer_cross(basket, scoped_pool) -> str:
315	        # c_shared's writer DRIFTS to B's claim, citing B's REGION of the shared source (it span-
316	        # grounds against the global row, but B's region is NOT c_shared's claim region).
317	        return f"{_b_span} [#ev:{_shared}:{_b_off[0]}-{_b_off[1]}]"
318	
319	    out_shared = _compose_section_per_basket(
320	        [c_shared], pool_shared, writer_fn=_writer_cross, verify_fn=verify_sentence_provenance,
321	    )
322	    rc = out_shared[0]
323	    if _b_span.rstrip(".") in rc:
324	        _fail("p1_1_shared_source",
325	              f"P1-1 VIOLATION: basket c_shared rendered basket B's claim region of the SHARED id; got {rc!r}")
326	    if _c_span.rstrip(".") not in rc or f"[#ev:{_shared}:{_c_off[0]}-{_c_off[1]}]" not in rc:
327	        _fail("p1_1_shared_kspan",
328	              f"c_shared must fall back to its OWN claim region ({_c_off}) of the shared source; got {rc!r}")
329	
330	    # ── GLOBAL RE-VERIFY (Codex core-gate P1-3): every emitted sentence must re-pass strict_verify
331	    # against the DOWNSTREAM (global) pool — proving the emitted tokens (composed re-anchor + verbatim
332	    # fallback offsets) resolve to the verified span globally, not just under the scoped pool. ──
333	    for tag, sent, pool in (("c1", r1, evidence_pool), ("c2", r2, evidence_pool),
334	                            ("c3", r3, evidence_pool), ("shared", rc, pool_shared)):
335	        for unit in [u for u in __import__("re").split(r"(?<=\])\s+(?=[A-Z0-9])", sent) if u.strip()]:
336	            gv = verify_sentence_provenance(unit, pool)
337	            if not bool(getattr(gv, "is_verified", False)):
338	                _fail("global_resolve",
339	                      f"{tag}: emitted sentence does NOT re-verify against the global pool "
340	                      f"(token mis-anchored downstream): {unit!r}")
341	
342	    # ── ISSUE #1279 — the two P1 faithfulness-hole fixtures (RED on the holed code, GREEN post-fix). ──
343	    _assert_p1_unsupported_member_excluded()
344	    _assert_p1b_claim_local_region()
345	
346	    print("PASS iarch011 PR-c verified-compose harness: "
347	          "c1 forced-fail -> OWN verbatim K-span (no foreign leak); "
348	          "c2 faithful compose PASSES strict_verify -> composed prose renders; "
349	          "c3 FOREIGN [#ev:c2] compose REJECTED under the basket-scoped pool (P1-2) -> c3's OWN K-span; "
350	          "SHARED-source cross-claim (basket cites a shared id for ANOTHER basket's claim) REJECTED via "
351	          "member-own-span scoping -> own claim-specific K-span (P1-1). "
352	          "ISSUE #1279 P1#1: an UNSUPPORTED in-basket member is EXCLUDED from the compose scoped pool + "
353	          "acceptance region (a writer citing it is REJECTED -> SUPPORTS K-span); "
354	          "ISSUE #1279 P1#2: _assemble_baskets binds direct_quote to the claim-LOCAL span (NOT the full "
355	          "row), so a cross-claim cite to another in-row claim is REJECTED -> own claim-local K-span. "
356	          "NEVER empty; strict_verify untouched (re-anchored sentence used); verbatim fallback basket-id-bound.")
357	    return 0
358	
359	
360	if __name__ == "__main__":
361	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-arch-011 PR-d behavioral replay-harness — the abstract/exec-summary (front) + conclusion
3	(end) layer, drafted LAST from the ALREADY-strict_verify-PASSED body (§-1.4, FAIL LOUD).
4	
5	WHAT THIS PROVES (behaviorally, on a REAL banked corpus_snapshot — not a synthetic fixture)
6	-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7	PR-d adds the front Abstract + end Conclusion as VERBATIM re-presentations of already-verified
8	body sentences — faithful BY IDENTITY (byte-equal to sentences that already passed strict_verify in
9	the body). It introduces NO new claim and NO new LLM call. (A deterministic cross-claim
10	author-summary gate was DESIGNED then CUT — see abstract_conclusion.py module docstring: bag-of-atoms
11	presence-checking provably cannot reject a RECOMBINATION of the body's own atoms, which is the exact
12	clinical-lethal class the gate targeted; rather than ship a green harness over an unsound gate, PR-d
13	ships verbatim-only and the synthesis machinery was removed. This harness therefore tests only what
14	SHIPS.) The harness drives the REAL production builders (``build_abstract`` / ``build_conclusion``)
15	over REAL SectionResult-shaped verified bodies derived from REAL banked corpus evidence, plus the REAL
16	frozen ``strict_verify`` and the REAL ``reconcile_report_against_verdicts`` / refilter — NOTHING is
17	monkeypatched. The corpus evidence text is loaded from a banked ``corpus_snapshot.json``
18	(``outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/*/`` resolved against the repo root + ancestors so it is found
19	from an isolated git worktree, or ``PG_PRD_CORPUS_DIR``); on a bare CI checkout it falls back to a
20	synthetic real-shaped fixture with a LOUD WARNING (never a silent pass-as-if-real).
21	
22	The assertions (non-zero exit on any miss):
23	  1. FAITHFUL BY IDENTITY: every sentence rendered in the abstract/conclusion is a VERBATIM body
24	     sentence (no fabricated/synthesized prose enters the summary), AND that body sentence re-passes
25	     the REAL strict_verify on its pre-resolve ``[#ev:]`` form (the path is genuinely verified).
26	  3. WEAK/UNVERIFIED EXCLUSION: a section that is NOT verified (gap-stub / sentences_verified=0 /
27	     dropped) contributes NO sentence to the abstract/conclusion — only verified body prose is lifted.
28	  4. FLAG-OFF BYTE-IDENTITY: ``PG_SYNTHESIS_ABSTRACT_CONCLUSION`` unset -> build_abstract/
29	     build_conclusion return "" so the assembled report.md is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the pre-PR-d render.
30	  5. EMPTY BODY -> the disclosed "insufficient verified evidence" line renders, NEVER fabricated filler.
31	
32	PLUS the post-gate REDACTION DUPLICATE landmine (brief P2-2): a verbatim abstract sentence whose
33	underlying body claim is flipped non-VERIFIED is redacted by the REAL
34	``reconcile_report_against_verdicts`` in the SAME pass as the body copy, and the REAL
35	``refilter_abstract_conclusion_block`` then drops the gap-stub'd block (no orphan, no empty heading).
36	
37	BOUNDED DEADLINE (§-1.4 hard requirement; the PR-c harness lacked this): the strict_verify leg runs
38	under an EXPLICIT internal wall-clock deadline in a worker thread. If the entailment judge is
39	UNREACHABLE and strict_verify would hang on the judge socket, the harness FAILS LOUD with a clear
40	non-zero exit + "judge unreachable" message — it NEVER hangs. It runs the assertions once on the
41	live-judge path (enforce) and once hermetically under ``PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off`` (reproducible
42	offline).
43	
44	Run: ``python scripts/iarch011_prd_abstract_conclusion_replay_harness.py`` -> exit 0 if every
45	assertion fires, non-zero + the failing assertion on any miss.
46	"""
47	from __future__ import annotations
48	
49	import json
50	import os
51	import re
52	import sys
53	import threading
54	from dataclasses import dataclass
55	from pathlib import Path
56	
57	# Make the repo root importable so the harness runs standalone (§-1.4). scripts/ is one level under it.
58	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
59	if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
60	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
61	
62	# Load C:/POLARIS/.env so the live judge runs when a key is present (brief: load_dotenv at startup).
63	try:
64	    from dotenv import load_dotenv  # type: ignore
65	
66	    load_dotenv(_REPO_ROOT / ".env")
67	except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — dotenv is optional; env may already be exported
68	    pass
69	
70	from src.polaris_graph.generator.abstract_conclusion import (  # noqa: E402
71	    _INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE,
72	    _harvest_abstract_sentences,
73	    _harvest_conclusion_sentences,
74	    build_abstract,
75	    build_conclusion,
76	    refilter_abstract_conclusion_block,
77	)
78	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import strict_verify  # noqa: E402
79	from src.polaris_graph.roles.report_redactor import (  # noqa: E402
80	    reconcile_report_against_verdicts,
81	)
82	
83	# Bounded internal deadline (seconds) for the single strict_verify leg that may call the live judge.
84	_STRICT_VERIFY_DEADLINE_S = 90.0
85	
86	
87	def _fail(case: str, detail: str) -> None:
88	    print(f"FAIL [{case}]: {detail}", file=sys.stderr)
89	    sys.exit(1)
90	
91	
92	# ── A minimal SectionResult stand-in (the builders read getattr fields only). ────────────────
93	@dataclass
94	class _FakeSection:
95	    title: str
96	    verified_text: str
97	    sentences_verified: int
98	    is_gap_stub: bool = False
99	    dropped_due_to_failure: bool = False
100	
101	
102	# ── SYNTHETIC REAL-SHAPED FALLBACK (LAW VI): only on a bare CI checkout with NO banked corpus.
103	# Real numeric clinical sentences (each carries a digit so strict_verify's decimal check fires).
104	_SYN = [
105	    ("Efficacy", "Semaglutide reduced major cardiovascular events by 20 percent in adults with obesity."),
106	    ("Glycaemic control", "Tirzepatide lowered HbA1c by 2.1 percentage points at 40 weeks versus placebo."),
107	    ("Heart failure", "Empagliflozin reduced heart failure hospitalization by 35 percent in adults."),
108	]
109	
110	
111	def _candidate_corpus_paths() -> list[Path]:
112	    """Banked corpus_snapshot.json candidate paths (LAW VI): ``PG_PRD_CORPUS_DIR``, the repo root, and
113	    EVERY ancestor of the repo root (so an isolated worktree finds the banked corpus in the main
114	    checkout above it), then ANY ``extracted/*/corpus_snapshot.json`` under each base."""
115	    bases: list[Path] = []
116	    env_dir = os.environ.get("PG_PRD_CORPUS_DIR")
117	    if env_dir:
118	        bases.append(Path(env_dir))
119	    bases.append(_REPO_ROOT)
120	    bases.extend(_REPO_ROOT.parents)
121	    out: list[Path] = []
122	    seen: set[str] = set()
123	    for base in bases:
124	        glob_root = base / "outputs" / "corpus_backups" / "extracted"
125	        try:
126	            globbed = sorted(glob_root.glob("*/corpus_snapshot.json"))
127	        except OSError:
128	            globbed = []
129	        for p in globbed:
130	            key = str(p)
131	            if key not in seen:
132	                seen.add(key)
133	                out.append(p)
134	    return out
135	
136	
137	def _load_corpus_rows(n: int = 3):
138	    """Load the first ``n`` SUBSTANTIVE evidence rows from a banked corpus_snapshot.json as
139	    ``[(evidence_id, direct_quote), ...]``. "Substantive" = quote >= 80 chars AND has a digit AND is
140	    distinct. Returns ``(rows, path)`` or ``(None, None)`` if none found / too few usable rows."""
141	    for path in _candidate_corpus_paths():
142	        try:
143	            if not path.is_file():
144	                continue
145	            with path.open(encoding="utf-8") as fh:
146	                data = json.load(fh)
147	        except (OSError, ValueError):
148	            continue
149	        ev_rows = data.get("evidence_for_gen")
150	        if not isinstance(ev_rows, list) or not ev_rows:
151	            ev_rows = data.get("evidence")
152	        if not isinstance(ev_rows, list) or not ev_rows:
153	            continue
154	        picked: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
155	        seen_quotes: set[str] = set()
156	        for row in ev_rows:
157	            if not isinstance(row, dict):
158	                continue
159	            eid = row.get("evidence_id") or row.get("id")
160	            quote = (row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("quote") or "").strip()
161	            if not eid or not quote:
162	                continue
163	            if len(quote) < 80 or not any(ch.isdigit() for ch in quote):
164	                continue
165	            if quote in seen_quotes:
166	                continue
167	            seen_quotes.add(quote)
168	            picked.append((str(eid), quote))
169	            if len(picked) >= n:
170	                break
171	        if len(picked) >= n:
172	            return picked, path
173	    return None, None
174	
175	
176	def _resolve_bodies():
177	    """Build the verified-body inputs. Prefer REAL banked corpus rows; on a bare checkout fall back
178	    to the synthetic real-shaped fixture with a LOUD warning. Returns
179	    ``(sections, evidence_pool, body_sentences_no_marker, source_kind, path)``.
180	
181	    Each section carries ONE verified sentence (the source's own words) + a rendered ``[N]`` citation
182	    (what the builders harvest); the matching ``evidence_pool`` row lets the harness drive the REAL
183	    strict_verify on the pre-resolve ``[#ev:eid:start-end]`` form and prove the re-presentation
184	    re-passes."""
185	    rows, path = _load_corpus_rows(3)
186	    if rows is not None:
187	        source_kind = "real"
188	        titles = ["Section A", "Section B", "Section C"]
189	        triples = [(titles[i], rows[i][0], rows[i][1]) for i in range(3)]
190	    else:
191	        print(
192	            "WARNING: no banked corpus found; using synthetic real-shaped fixture "
193	            "(set PG_PRD_CORPUS_DIR or run from a checkout with "
194	            "outputs/corpus_backups/extracted/*/corpus_snapshot.json to replay REAL evidence).",
195	            file=sys.stderr,
196	        )
197	        source_kind = "synthetic"
198	        path = None
199	        triples = [(_SYN[i][0], f"ev_b{i + 1}", _SYN[i][1]) for i in range(3)]
200	
201	    sections: list[_FakeSection] = []
202	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict] = {}
203	    body_sentences_no_marker: list[str] = []
204	    for idx, (title, eid, quote) in enumerate(triples, start=1):
205	        body_sentence = f"{quote} [{idx}]"
206	        sections.append(
207	            _FakeSection(title=title, verified_text=body_sentence, sentences_verified=1)
208	        )
209	        evidence_pool[eid] = {
210	            "source_url": f"https://example.org/{eid}",
211	            "tier": "T1",
212	            "direct_quote": quote,
213	            "statement": quote,
214	            "evidence_id": eid,
215	        }
216	        body_sentences_no_marker.append(quote)
217	    return sections, evidence_pool, body_sentences_no_marker, source_kind, path
218	
219	
220	def _strict_verify_with_deadline(draft_text: str, evidence_pool: dict):
221	    """Run strict_verify under an EXPLICIT internal wall-clock deadline in a worker thread. If the
222	    entailment judge is UNREACHABLE and strict_verify would HANG on the judge socket, this returns a
223	    timeout marker so the caller FAILS LOUD (non-zero exit + "judge unreachable") — it NEVER hangs
224	    (§-1.4 hard requirement). Returns (report, error) where exactly one is non-None; ("__timeout__"
225	    error on deadline)."""
226	    box: dict[str, object] = {}
227	
228	    def _worker() -> None:
229	        try:
230	            box["report"] = strict_verify(draft_text, evidence_pool)
231	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — surface, never swallow
232	            box["error"] = exc
233	
234	    t = threading.Thread(target=_worker, daemon=True)
235	    t.start()
236	    t.join(_STRICT_VERIFY_DEADLINE_S)
237	    if t.is_alive():
238	        return None, "__timeout__"
239	    if "error" in box:
240	        return None, box["error"]
241	    return box.get("report"), None
242	
243	
244	def _run_assertions(entailment_mode: str, sections, evidence_pool, body_sentences) -> dict:
245	    """Run the assertions + the redaction landmine under a given PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT mode.
246	    Returns a status dict; calls _fail (non-zero exit) on any miss."""
247	    os.environ["PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT"] = entailment_mode
248	    os.environ["PG_SYNTHESIS_ABSTRACT_CONCLUSION"] = "1"
249	
250	    abstract_md = build_abstract(sections)
251	    conclusion_md = build_conclusion(sections)
252	    if not abstract_md.strip() or not conclusion_md.strip():
253	        _fail("build", f"[{entailment_mode}] build_abstract/build_conclusion returned empty under "
254	                       "PG_SYNTHESIS_ABSTRACT_CONCLUSION=1 (the feature did not fire).")
255	    if "## Abstract" not in abstract_md or "## Conclusion" not in conclusion_md:
256	        _fail("build", f"[{entailment_mode}] abstract/conclusion block headers absent.")
257	
258	    # ── ASSERTION 1a — FAITHFUL BY IDENTITY: every sentence the builders lift into the abstract/
259	    # conclusion is a VERBATIM substring of the verified body (the builders harvest sentence-pieces of
260	    # SectionResult.verified_text). If any rendered sentence is NOT a body substring, synthesized/
261	    # fabricated prose entered the summary. Whitespace-normalize both sides; drop the trailing [N]. ──
262	    nbody = " ".join(" ".join(s.verified_text.split()) for s in sections)
263	    for block_name, block_md, harvested in (
264	        ("abstract", abstract_md, _harvest_abstract_sentences(sections)),
265	        ("conclusion", conclusion_md, _harvest_conclusion_sentences(sections)),
266	    ):
267	        if not harvested:
268	            _fail("assert1_identity", f"[{entailment_mode}] the {block_name} harvested NO verified "
269	                  "body sentence (the feature did not lift any finding).")
270	        # (a) every harvested sentence is a verbatim body substring (no fabricated/synthesized prose).
271	        for sent in harvested:
272	            core = " ".join(sent.split(" [")[0].split())  # drop trailing [N] citation + normalize ws
273	            if core and core not in nbody:
274	                _fail("assert1_identity", f"[{entailment_mode}] a rendered {block_name} sentence is "
275	                      f"NOT a verbatim body substring (synthesized/fabricated content): {sent!r}")
276	        # (b) the FINAL RENDERED block findings line equals EXACTLY the harvested verbatim sentences
277	        #     joined — i.e. nothing other than verbatim body sentences reaches the rendered artifact
278	        #     (parses the actual abstract_md/conclusion_md, not just the harvester output). ──
279	        rendered_findings = " ".join(
280	            ln.strip() for ln in block_md.splitlines()
281	            if ln.strip() and not ln.strip().startswith("#") and not ln.strip().startswith("_")
282	        )
283	        if " ".join(rendered_findings.split()) != " ".join(" ".join(harvested).split()):
284	            _fail("assert1_identity", f"[{entailment_mode}] the rendered {block_name} findings line is "
285	                  "NOT exactly the harvested verbatim body sentences (extra/synthesized content reached "
286	                  f"the rendered artifact): rendered={rendered_findings!r}")
287	
288	    # ── ASSERTION 1b — GENUINELY verified: EVERY rendered (harvested) abstract/conclusion sentence
289	    # re-passes the REAL strict_verify on its pre-resolve provenance form (Codex iter-2 P2-2: every
290	    # rendered sentence, not just the first). Each harvested sentence is located at its EXACT span in the
291	    # source evidence quote, and the [#ev:] token is attached with NO space — the PRODUCTION token format
292	    # ("...trial.[#ev:...]"); a space lets strict_verify's splitter cut at the sentence-final period and
293	    # orphan the token. Bounded-deadline so it FAILS LOUD if the judge is unreachable, never hangs. ──
294	    _rendered: list[str] = []
295	    _seen_r: set[str] = set()
296	    for sent in _harvest_abstract_sentences(sections) + _harvest_conclusion_sentences(sections):
297	        core = re.sub(r"\s*\[\d+\]\s*$", "", sent).strip()  # drop the trailing [N] citation
298	        if core and core not in _seen_r:
299	            _seen_r.add(core)
300	            _rendered.append(core)
301	    if not _rendered:
302	        _fail("assert1_verbatim_repass", f"[{entailment_mode}] no rendered sentence to re-verify "
303	              "(the abstract/conclusion lifted nothing).")
304	    for core in _rendered:
305	        draft = None
306	        for eid, row in evidence_pool.items():
307	            pos = row["direct_quote"].find(core)
308	            if pos >= 0:
309	                draft = f"{core}[#ev:{eid}:{pos}-{pos + len(core)}]"  # NO space (production token format)
310	                break
311	        if draft is None:  # identity (1a) already guarantees this; defensive
